column1: # PocketDNS partition table  (target: 4 MB flash ESP32 DevKit V1)
# This file is the MAP of the chip's flash memory. The bootloader reads it
# to know where everything lives. We design it ONCE
# never have to repartition later (repartitioning erases the whole chip).
# Columns:  Name
# Notes:
#  - App partitions MUST start on a 0x10000 (64 KB) boundary.
#  - We reserve TWO app slots (ota_0 / ota_1) so a future OTA update can be
#    written to the inactive slot and swapped atomically. If it bricks
#    bootloader rolls back. That safety needs the second slot to exist now.
#  - The data partition is labelled "littlefs" but uses subtype "spiffs".
#    Why: the LittleFS component matches the partition by its LABEL
#    subtype
column2: and "spiffs" is a subtype every ESP-IDF version accepts. This

column1: #    avoids a build error on toolchains that don't know a "littlefs" subtype.
#  - "coredump" is reserved now (64 KB) so that when we enable crash dumps in
#    the debugging phase
column2: the space already exists. Left unused for Phase 1.

column1: # 4 MB = 0x400000. Every byte below is accounted for
# Name
nvs
otadata
phy_init
ota_0
ota_1
littlefs
coredump
